benchmark: idf_pow (IDF term-specificity emphasis) — both arms, with synaptic re-run in the same pass
synaptic_driver: synaptic's OWN eval.run_all.run_public_dataset, embedder=None, reranker=None — the same driver eval/public_bench.py uses
why_rerun: Two reasons. (1) The idf_pow figures quoted in the docs were measured before the English S-stemmer and the Korean copula-interrogative fix; an ablation whose arms run code that no longer ships is not an ablation. (2) The first re-run compared OmniFuse's two arms against synaptic numbers COPIED from older result files. Comparing a fresh measurement to a remembered one is not a head-to-head. synaptic is now re-ingested and re-queried per dataset.
recorded_vs_remeasured_synaptic: All 12 reproduce the recorded numbers exactly, and finreg's 0.7039 / 56 does too. The old synaptic column was right; it just had not been earned in this pass.

finreg single-hop.source: eval/compare_synaptic.py --synaptic-graph (re-run 2026-07-10; synaptic 0.7039 / multi-hop 56, identical to the recorded number). multi-hop strict-solved: idf_pow=1.0 -> 108, idf_pow=1.5 -> 107, synaptic 56.

headline.reading: Near zero (+0.0065 across 13 datasets). idf_pow=1.5 is NOT 'strictly additive' — an earlier claim in the docs, now retracted. It buys AutoRAG (+0.0143), HotPotQA-200 (+0.0119), Ko-StrategyQA (+0.0062), SciFact, PublicHealthQA and XPQA; it costs MIRACL-ko (-0.0196), finreg (-0.0133, and one multi-hop query: 108 -> 107), NFCorpus (-0.0054), Allganize-ko (-0.0017) and KLUE (-0.0003).

the_uncomfortable_part.fact: At idf_pow=1.0 OmniFuse still beats synaptic on 12 of the 13 lexical datasets (11/12 in idf_pow_bench, plus finreg). The only loss is Ko-StrategyQA, by 0.0006 MRR. Measured against a synaptic re-run, not a recorded number.
the_uncomfortable_part.scale: Ko-StrategyQA has 592 queries, so one query moving from rank 2 to rank 1 is worth 0.0008 MRR. The margin that decides '15/15 vs 14/15' is smaller than a single query.
the_uncomfortable_part.honesty: The shipped default therefore does not rest on a suite-wide gain — it rests on one dataset by less than one query, while measurably costing finreg and MIRACL. We keep 1.5 because the win holds across the whole band p in [1.3, 2.0] (Ko-StrategyQA wins from p>=1.1: 0.6452 / 0.6466 / 0.6500 / 0.6496 / 0.6487 / 0.6470 at 1.1/1.2/1.3/1.5/1.7/2.0), so 1.5 is a mid-band choice rather than a knife-edge fit. But readers deserve the number, and idf_pow=1.0 remains the right call on heavily multi-relevant corpora.

is_the_entity_burial_rationale_real.prediction: If idf_pow works by rescuing a rare discriminative term buried under common ones, then on queries it RESCUES the gold document should match a rarer query term, and fewer of them, than the wrong top-1; on queries it BREAKS the reverse should hold.
is_the_entity_burial_rationale_real.verdict: Supported. The sign of (gold max-IDF - wrong-top-1 max-IDF) FLIPS between the two groups (+0.14 when it rescues, -0.78 when it breaks), and the gold consistently matches fewer terms. idf_pow bets on rarity: it wins when the gold document is the rare-term holder and loses when it is not. That is a mechanism, not a story — but it is also why the net effect is a wash.

add_되_to_KO_SUFFIX.idea: The rarest query terms on Ko-StrategyQA are stemmer residues, not entities ('있나' idf 7.63, '#연속되' 8.73, '벌은' 7.43). _KO_SUFFIX contains 하 but not 되 — the same closed class, one half missing — so 연속되나요 stops at '연속되'.
add_되_to_KO_SUFFIX.result: Real but immaterial. It fixes 연속되 -> 연속, yet Ko-StrategyQA at p=1.0 moves 0.6434 -> 0.6435 (still a loss) and at p=1.5 drops 0.6496 -> 0.6490. AutoRAG/KLUE/PublicHealthQA/Allganize unchanged. Not shipped.
add_되_to_KO_SUFFIX.why_it_failed: The other residues are not reachable: '있나요' -> '있나' stops at the 2-char stem floor, and '사용되었다' has no listed trailing ending at all. The residue theory does not explain the 0.0006 gap.

per_query_diff_at_p1.0_vs_synaptic.question: is Ko-StrategyQA's 0.0006 loss at p=1.0 a clustered, fixable defect (like MIRACL's copula) or diffuse disagreement?
per_query_diff_at_p1.0_vs_synaptic.method: per-query reciprocal_rank diff, omni@idf_pow=1.0 vs synaptic re-run through its own graph.search (FTS), 592 queries
per_query_diff_at_p1.0_vs_synaptic.loss_shape: the visible losses ARE entity-burial: a Korean query naming a transliterated entity (이기 팝 -> Iggy Pop, 소니 플레이스테이션 -> PlayStation, LinkedIn) whose gold doc holds the rare term, out-summed at p=1.0 by documents matching several common words. That is precisely the failure idf_pow=1.5 corrects — which is why 1.5 wins this set.
per_query_diff_at_p1.0_vs_synaptic.verdict: No clustered defect. The two systems disagree on 186 of 592 queries and split them almost exactly evenly (RR mass 36.49 vs 36.14); the 0.0006 margin is the residue of a coin-flip-even disagreement, not a bug with a root cause. Unlike MIRACL (29 losses concentrated on one junk title -> fixed by the copula ending), there is nothing principled to fix here — any change targeted at flipping this margin would be fitting to test labels. The shipped disclosure (keep idf_pow=1.5, band-robust, margin stated) is the final state.
